intro: The most visible split between Plaid and Didit in our data is developer experience: Plaid scores 5/5, Didit scores 4/5. Plaid is Financial data network connecting apps to 12,000+ banks for account verification, income data, and identity checks; it fits best for Fintech, Marketplace. Didit is Free KYC verification API with document scanning, NFC chip reading, and facial biometric matching for identity checks; it fits best for Fintech, Marketplace. Pick Plaid for Fintech, Marketplace; pick Didit for Fintech, Marketplace. Plaid is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements. Didit is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements. Honest trade-offs: Plaid is weak for consumer apps without regulatory requirements, and Didit is weak for consumer apps without regulatory requirements — match to your real scenario. Both sit under the KYC / KYB / AML API umbrella, so your final call depends on pricing transparency, integration effort, and which listed not-for situations you can actually tolerate.
quick_take: Plaid is for fintech, marketplace; Didit is for fintech, marketplace; decide based on developer experience.
verdict_a: Choose Plaid if developer experience rated 5/5 is good enough and your fit is fintech, marketplace.
verdict_b: Choose Didit if developer experience rated 4/5 matches your team's needs and your fit is fintech, marketplace.

faq.a: A direct comparison depends on your usage profile and which pricing model fits better. Plaid uses subscription pricing with no free tier published. Didit uses subscription pricing starting at $666/mo.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Plaid to Didit?
faq.a: Migration is workable but not trivial, since both sit around medium lock-in. Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

Plaid scores higher on developer experience (5/5 vs 4/5 for Didit). Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is Didit a good alternative to Plaid?
faq.a: Didit sits in the same category as Plaid, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. Didit is best fit for fintech, marketplace. Note: Didit is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements.
